CPI day, pre-QOpEx - Intraday post (18/Dec)

Weekly post:

OpEx, VIX expo, CPI and BoJ meeting - Weekly post (15-19/Dec)

In the weekly post I predicted that we will see cooler datas that market will embrace as market wants stimulus and easing cycle. Admin wants this. Do not forget who is the Treasury Secretary… this is the “Bessent put” that I keep referring to since April: manipulating the equity/yields correlation in favor for the coming datas to sustain growth even on paper. Core CPI (the one that matters for the Fed) came out cooler than expected.

We are in an economic cold war. Never forget this.

Let’s break down the positioning for today including tomorrow AM expo…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

I took the AM expo effects into account…Net speed profile is positive here. Customers sell downside vol and upside vol, but roughly 7.6x more upside vol is sold, than downside, making the speed profile net positive, and downside vomma is heavily sold.

The centroid is at 6729.78 - 6739.16, this is the most important area.Locally it look like it is hard to break bcs it coincidences with some local speed flips too…

Downside pivot is at 6697.57with downside target of 6665 and secondary of 6630/6640With high vol, it can overshoot towards 6618 but reversion can be expected towards 6665.The model estimates that if the vol catches up and flushes the downside short vols, the max downside target is 6592.56But this is a left-tail scenario.

Upside pivot is at 6790.45with target of 6839.40There is way more chance to break above the pivot during the AM session. After that it will be hard, and spot will be pinned most likely to 6750 (with +- 30 pts rangebound). Above 6810/15 vol selling kicks in, and spot can stabilize towards the upside target. Margin of error is 6848, low chance to break it, market needs a squeeze before 2PM. And then it can climb up to 6880

Momentum day.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
